title: trifolium repens subsp. repens
intro: Trifolium repens subsp. repens, commonly known as white clover, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family that is native to Europe, Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of white clover include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting digestion, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. White clover can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used as a food source for livestock and as a cover crop in agriculture.
